title: rich italian nectarine puff pastry tart
description: a rich, crumbly, and quick-fix italian dish featuring nectarines, lamination-puff, and a fodmap-friendly take on the classic vitello-tonnato recipe.

ingredients.name: 1 sheet of puff pastry, thawed
ingredients.notes: frozen puff pastry can be used if thawed in the refrigerator overnight

ingredients.name: 2 large nectarines
ingredients.notes: nectarines should be ripe but firm

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: fodmap-friendly garlic preparation: cook garlic lightly before adding to the tart

instructions: roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm) and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
in a small bowl, mix together the granulated sugar, garlic, basil, and ricotta cheese. Set aside.
slice the nectarines into 1/4 inch (6 mm) thick wedges and arrange them on top of the puff pastry in a spiral pattern, leaving a 1 inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
sprinkle the sugar, garlic, basil, and ricotta cheese mixture evenly over the nectarines.
fold the edges of the puff pastry up over the nectarines, pressing gently to seal.
brush the egg wash over the pastry to give it a golden glaze.
bake the tart for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the nectarines are tender.
remove the tart from the oven and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
serve warm, garnished with additional basil leaves if desired.
